verse_specific_anchors: yadṛcchayā — unsought; the operative word marking the verse
svarga-dvāram apāvṛtam — the door wide open; mapped to 'gate has opened, walking through it'
sukhinaḥ kṣatriyāḥ — fortunate kṣatriyas; mapped to 'lucky operator' (descriptive, not prescriptive)
verdict_reason: The engineering analog anchors specifically on yadṛcchayā. The auth-rewrite scenario is uniquely the unsought-arrival analog: the convergence of three independent events (Sentry-flagged incident + new VP + contractor-exit-frees-budget) the engineer did not engineer. Other verses in the duty block cannot be substituted: 2.31 is about role-bound context (which is about why the duty is yours, not why the moment is now), 2.33 is about mid-execution-quitting, 2.47 is about disowning fruits. Only 2.32 names the unsought-arrival-as-marker argument. The verse cannot be force-fit elsewhere because the convergence-of-three-independent-events shape is specifically the yadṛcchayā analog. PASS on both 7.6 and 3.5.
